text: Has Big Ben ever gone wrong?
When you visit London, one of the first things you will see is Big Ben,
zh: 当你游览伦敦时,最先看到的景象之一就是大本钟,

text: the famous clock which can be heard all over the world on the B.B.C.
zh: 这座钟因在英国广播公司的广播中传遍世界而闻名。

text: If the Houses of Parliament had not been burned down in 1834,
zh: 要不是1834年国会大厦被烧毁,

text: the great clock would never have been erected.
zh: 这座大钟就绝不会被建造起来。

text: Big Ben takes its name from Sir Benjamin Hall who was responsible for the making of the clock when the new Houses of Parliament were being built.
zh: 大本钟得名于本杰明·霍尔爵士,他在新国会大厦建造期间负责这座钟的制造工作。

text: It is not only of immense size, but is extremely accurate as well.
zh: 它不仅体积庞大,而且报时极其精准。

text: Officials from Greenwich Observatory have the clock checked twice a day.
zh: 格林尼治天文台的官员每天要对这座钟进行两次校验。

text: On the B.B.C. you can hear the clock when it is actually striking because microphones are connected to the clock tower.
zh: 在英国广播公司的节目中,你能听到大本钟真实敲响的声音,因为麦克风被连接到了钟楼上。

text: Big Ben has rarely gone wrong.
Once, however, it failed to give the correct time.
zh: 然而有一次,它却没能报出正确的时间。

text: A painter who had been working on the tower hung a pot of paint on one of the hands and slowed it down!
zh: 一位在钟楼上工作的油漆工把一桶油漆挂在了其中一根指针上,结果拖慢了它的走动!
